file_name: SLOVAKWIRELESSFINANCECOBV_03_28_2001-EX-4.(B)(II).3-Maintenance and support contract for SICAP(R) modules.txt

perturbation.original_text: Response time: The time between the receipt of the notification by Sicap Ltd of an incident or problem and the initiation by the HotLine of a solution for the incident or problem.
perturbation.changed_text: Response time: The time between the receipt of the notification by Sicap Ltd of an incident or problem and the acknowledgement of reciept by the HotLine.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Response time' refers to 'the initiation by the HotLine of a solution'. This implies a proactive approach to resolving issues. However, the modified definition states 'acknowledgement of reciept by the HotLine.' This reduces the commitment to merely acknowledging the issue, thereby contradicting the expectation of problem resolution that's built into the original text and sections describing response times and priorities. This is contradicting the commitment that Sicap Ltd has of solving the issue, since a response time can vary a lot.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Response time for hotline The Response Time during the HotLine availability hours shall be the following: -------------------------------------------------------------------------- Priority of reques Availability Response Time -------------------------------------------------------------------------- emergency 24 hours, 7 days a week 4 hours -------------------------------------------------------------------------- high during working hours 4 hours -------------------------------------------------------------------------- medium during working hours next working day -------------------------------------------------------------------------- low during working hours day after next working day -------------------------------------------------------------------------- It is up to EuroTel to decide which priority occurs. In case of an obvious misuse, Sicap Ltd has the right to charge the costs. Sicap Ltd will perform every possibility to make the system running as soon as possible. Staff of EuroTel must be on-site on request of Sicap Ltd.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Response time' included 'the initiation by the HotLine of a solution,' implying a commitment to problem resolution. The changed definition replaces this with 'acknowledgement of reciept by the HotLine,' which only commits to acknowledging the issue. This contradicts the contradicted text (Section 3.1), which specifies response times for different priority levels (e.g., 'emergency 24 hours, 7 days a week 4 hours'), implying a solution is expected within that timeframe. Merely acknowledging receipt does not fulfill the obligation to initiate a solution within the specified response times, creating a strong inconsistency and potential unenforceability. The change fundamentally alters the expected action within the stated response time, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: After the initial period of two years, the maintenance and support contract shall be automatically renewed for a period of one year on each renewal date, unless one of the parties terminates the maintenance and support contract through written notification to the other party in the form of a registered letter with proof of receipt, at least six (6) weeks prior to the renewal date.
perturbation.changed_text: After the initial period of two years, the maintenance and support contract may be renewed, subject to Sicap Ltd's discretion, for a period of one year on each renewal date. EuroTel must notify Sicap Ltd of its intent to renew at least six (6) weeks prior to the renewal date; failure to do so will result in automatic termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the contract auto-renews unless either party terminates it with written notice. The modified version changes this to say renewal is at Sicap Ltd's discretion. Furthermore, it places the onus solely on EuroTel to initiate the renewal process and penalizes them with automatic termination if they don't act in time. This creates a contradiction, as the contract was originally structured to continue automatically. This change gives Sicap Ltd unilateral control over renewal, contradicting the original mutual renewal process, and the fact that it may be renewed and not 'shall' is a huge change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.5 Term and termination This contract replaces in full the Annex 7 "Maintenance and Support for SICAP(R) modules PPB, VCA and OTA and Sun Hardware" which is an integral part of the original Swisscom PURCHASE & LICENSE CONTRACT, made effective on 06 April 1998. It shall be effective on 06 October 2000 and shall have a duration of two years. The prices remain valid for the first 12 months. After this period, Sicap Ltd reserves the right to adjust the prices annually. The offer must be submitted three (3) months in advance and EuroTel may decide within one (1) month to prolong or terminate this maintenance and support contract. If EuroTel doesn't react in writing, the new offer shall be considered as accepted. After the initial period of two years, the maintenance and support contract may be renewed, subject to Sicap Ltd's discretion, for a period of one year on each renewal date. EuroTel must notify Sicap Ltd of its intent to renew at least six (6) weeks prior to the renewal date; failure to do so will result in automatic termination. There shall be no reimbursement of any paid fee. In case of a substantial breach of this maintenance and support contract by either party, which is not remedied within thirty days from the other party's notice thereof, the other party shall have the right to terminate this maintenance and support contract with immediate effect without juridical procedures. The termination or expiration of the maintenance and support contract for any reason whatever shall be without prejudice to any other right or obligation of any party hereto in respect of this maintenance and support contract which have arisen prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the contract auto-renews unless terminated with written notice. The changed text makes renewal subject to Sicap Ltd's discretion and places the onus on EuroTel to initiate the renewal, with failure leading to automatic termination. This contradicts the original structure where renewal was automatic, giving Sicap Ltd unilateral control. Furthermore, the contradicted text in 7.5 discusses renewal and termination, and the changed text alters the renewal process described within this section, causing a direct conflict. The shift from 'shall be automatically renewed' to 'may be renewed, subject to Sicap Ltd's discretion' is a fundamental change that breaks the original agreement's renewal process.

perturbation.original_text: Sicap Ltd's liability for indirect loss, including consequential loss, loss of profit, lost savings and loss caused by interruption of operations is excluded. No right to damages shall ever arise unless EuroTel reports the loss to Sicap Ltd in writing as soon as possible after it has arisen.
perturbation.changed_text: Sicap Ltd's liability for any loss, whether direct or indirect, is limited to the total fees paid by EuroTel under this contract during the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the loss. EuroTel must report any loss to Sicap Ltd in writing within 30 days of the event; failure to do so waives any right to claim damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause excludes liability for indirect losses entirely. The modified version introduces a limit on liability—to the total fees paid in the last 12 months—and extends it to cover *any* loss. This directly contradicts the original blanket exclusion of indirect losses and introduces the possibility of claiming for direct losses, something not previously mentioned. Also the the limitation period from 'as soon as possible' to '30 days' is a big shift of the burden of the other party, this creates uncertainty about how long Eurotel can claim damages. Also the penalty of 'waiving any right to claim damages' also creates uncertainty about how the damaged can be resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.6 Liability Sicap Ltd's liability for any loss, whether direct or indirect, is limited to the total fees paid by EuroTel under this contract during the 12 months preceding the event giving rise to the loss. EuroTel must report any loss to Sicap Ltd in writing within 30 days of the event; failure to do so waives any right to claim damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text excludes liability for indirect losses, while the changed text limits liability for *any* loss (direct or indirect) to fees paid in the preceding 12 months. This directly contradicts the complete exclusion of indirect losses. The contradicted text, which is the same as the Changed Text, highlights this contradiction by asserting the limitation of liability for all losses, contrasting with the original exclusion of indirect losses. The shift from 'as soon as possible' to '30 days' for reporting losses introduces a significant change in the limitation period, creating uncertainty and contradiction about the time frame for claiming damages. The penalty of 'waiving any right to claim damages' for failure to report within 30 days introduces further contradiction and uncertainty regarding the resolution of damages.
